## FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQs)

- ### Do signers need an account to sign the documents I send them via Zoho Sign?


No, signers do not need an account or a subscription to sign the documents you send them via Zoho Sign. Every envelope is sent to the signers in an email containing an exclusive signing link that allows them to access, view, and digitally sign the documents. An account or a subscription is only required for those in your organization who will use Zoho Sign to self-sign business documents or send them out for signature.

- ### How does the 14-day free trial work?


Upon signing up, you get a 14-day free trial with access to the Enterprise plan by default. You are not required to enter your credit card details to avail this trial. You can switch freely between the different plans during the trial period to explore and understand their respective features and limitations. During this period, you can add a maximum of 10 users and get up to 50 envelopes signed (limited to 3 recipients per envelope and 5 envelopes per day). Documents signed during the free trial will carry a watermark—move to the Free plan or subscribe to a paid plan to remove the watermark.

- ### What happens when the trial period ends?


After the 14-day trial period, if you have not upgraded to a paid plan, you will be automatically moved to the Free plan, with all your data intact. You may then subscribe to our general plans / [API-only plan](https://www.zoho.com/sign/pricing-api.html) at any time.

- ### How can I upgrade my account?


The portal owner i.e. the organization administrator who is the payment owner is the only person who can upgrade your account. If you are the portal owner, log in to Zoho Sign, navigate to Settings >> Subscription details >> Upgrade and the wizard will guide you through the process.

- ### Whom should I contact for sales-related questions?


If you have questions on the pricing, please write to us at [sales@zohocorp.com](mailto:sales@zohocorp.com).

- ### Can I upgrade/downgrade/cancel my account at anytime?


Yes. Zoho Sign is a pay-as-you-go service so you can upgrade, downgrade, or cancel at anytime.

- ### What is an envelope?


An envelope is an individual digital signature workflow or request initiated in Zoho Sign, either manually from the app interface or using other options such as API, mobile SDKs, automations, custom functions, and integrations across both Zoho and third-party apps. Each envelope can contain multiple documents and can be sent to multiple recipients with different roles in a specified order. Whenever a digital signature workflow is executed or an envelope is sent for signature, we say that a signature request has been raised or generated. In bulk send and SignForms, a unique envelope is generated for every recipient in the uploaded CSV file and every response received respectively.

- ### When should I purchase Zoho Sign credits?


[Zoho Sign credits](https://www.zoho.com/sign/zoho-sign-credits.html) are a separate add-on you can purchase in denominations of 500 at US$0 _.10_US$0.1 per credit. You should purchase them if you plan to use the features that consume them. These credits are consumed to absorb the additional cost incurred by high volume automations or third-party integrations involving separate service costs. If you have subscribed to the Enterprise plan, you will receive complimentary credits on a monthly basis proportionate to the count of user licenses you have purchased. Automations across Zoho apps will first consume any [complimentary credits](https://www.zoho.com/sign/automation-credits.html) available in your account and will require purchase of additional Zoho Sign credits only upon their exhaustion. If you have subscribed to the [API-only plan](https://www.zoho.com/sign/pricing-api.html), you will need to purchase Zoho Sign credits to get documents signed as there is no other associated subscription cost. Envelopes sent via API or third-party apps always consume Zoho Sign credits irrespective of your subscription plan.

## Overview of Zoho Sign pricing

[Zoho Sign](https://www.capterra.com/p/215876/Zoho-Sign/) is a [digital signature software](https://www.capterra.com/digital-signature-software/) developed by Zoho that offers a streamlined alternative to traditional paper signatures, simplifying the process of signing and managing electronic documents. Zoho Sign sends documents inside digital ‘envelopes’ that include additional information such as an expiration date for the signature or any notes that need to be attached.

It offers various subscription pricing tiers:

- Free

- Standard

- Professional

- Enterprise

- API-only

- Custom


The paid plans charge a flat fee per user, per month, with both annual and monthly billing available. Zoho provides different features in each of these tiers, letting clients choose the best plan for their business needs and objectives. Additionally, the platform provides an application programming interface (API)-only pricing model and custom pricing for large enterprises with unique needs.

We dug into our data to help you understand the different pricing plans available, identify the critical distinctions between each plan, and choose the right one based on your team size and needs. Let’s compare these plans in detail:

## Cost breakdown

Zoho Sign’s flexible pricing structure can fit the needs of many different clients—everyone from small businesses to multinational enterprises. This multi-tiered approach allows companies to easily scale their usage of Zoho Sign as their needs evolve.

### Starting price

Zoho Sign’s **Free tier** is free of charge for a single user, who can send up to five envelopes per month. This tier is an excellent option for solopreneurs and small businesses who want to try digital signature services before making a financial commitment.

The software’s **Standard tier** costs $10 per user, per month if billed annually, or $12 per user if billed monthly. This tier allows for multiple users, who can each send up to 25 envelopes per month and have access to advanced features such as document timestamping and signer identification. As a result, this tier is a good choice for small and midsized businesses (SMBs) whose business requirements don’t fit inside the constraints of the Free plan.

Zoho Sign’s **Professional tier** costs $16 per user, per month if billed annually, or $20 per user on the monthly plan. The Professional tier lets users send an unlimited amount of envelopes, adds branding and customization, and supports multiple languages—making it ideal for larger businesses that rely heavily on electronic signatures or operate in different countries.

The software’s **Enterprise tier** costs $22 per user, per month if billed annually, and $28 per user when paying from month to month. This tier offers bulk sending and signing, compliance with regulatory frameworks, and SignForms (a self-service feature for collecting signatures from a public URL). As the name suggests, the Enterprise tier is suitable for large enterprises or businesses in certain industries that need to comply with strict data privacy and security regulations.

Zoho Sign also offers an **API-only pricing model**, which charges a flat rate of $0.50 per envelope with no extra financial cost. This tier includes all the features of the Enterprise plan, but it requires users to configure documents directly via the Zoho Sign APIs. As a result, the API pricing model is a good option for businesses with in-depth technical skills and customers whose electronic signature needs may change dramatically from month to month.

Finally, the software offers a **Custom pricing tier** for businesses that have unique requirements. Interested customers will need to contact the Zoho sales team for a quote.

In addition to subscribing to Zoho Sign as a standalone service, businesses also have access to the **Zoho One software suite**, which combines dozens of enterprise applications under one roof. Zoho One is available for $90 per user, per month (if billed annually), or at a discounted $37 per user, per month when buying licenses for every employee in an organization.

### Billing options

Zoho Sign offers both monthly and annual payment options for most of its paid tiers. Making annual payments offers discounts compared to paying monthly—between roughly 15% and 25% for each plan. The exceptions are the API pricing plan (which charges a flat fee per envelope) and the Custom plan (which doesn’t specify whether it charges a monthly or annual fee).

Each of these billing options will appeal to different customers. A monthly subscription allows for flexibility, letting businesses try the product before they sign an annual commitment or upgrade to a more expensive tier. An annual subscription is the right choice for budget-conscious businesses that are sure they’ll need the product for at least the next year. Finally, a per-envelope pricing model is good for businesses with uncertain or variable needs at different times of year.

### Feature differentiation

Comparing features will help illuminate the differences between the various Zoho Sign payment plans:

- **Number of users:** The Free plan allows for a single user only, while the other plans allow for as many users as you pay for.

- **Number of envelopes:** Free tier users can only send up to five envelopes per month. Standard tier users can send up to 25 envelopes per month, per user, while other tiers allow for unlimited envelopes.

- **Integrations:** The Free tier only allows for basic integrations with other Zoho applications. The Standard and Professional tiers provide advanced Zoho integrations and basic third-party integrations. Finally, the Enterprise tier adds advanced third-party integrations.

- **Advanced features:** The Free tier allows for signing with a USB token for a digital signature certificate (DSC). The Standard and Professional tiers add qualified timestamping, advanced electronic signatures (AES), and qualified electronic signatures (QES) via Swisscom. Finally, the Enterprise tier also includes blockchain timestamping and QES via Uanataca and InfoCert.

## Total cost of ownership

When evaluating Zoho Sign for your document signature needs, it’s important to consider the total cost of ownership (TCO), including upfront costs and any additional one-off or recurring costs. Here’s a breakdown to help you understand all potential expenses:

### Upfront costs

Upfront costs are the initial expenses that you pay when buying or subscribing to software—such as software licensing, setup, customization and configuration, data migration, and onboarding and training. The Zoho Sign website says that you may need to pay additional local taxes, such as a value-added tax or a goods & services tax.

Beyond the subscription prices, Zoho Sign’s pricing page does not mention any specific upfront costs for any of the software plans. This is likely because Zoho Sign is available only as a cloud-based service, not on-premise. Because cloud software runs on the provider’s servers, customers are generally not responsible for the costs of setup or installation.

### Additional costs to consider

Thus far, we haven’t mentioned Zoho Sign credits, a significant additional cost for Zoho Sign users. These are prepaid add-ons purchased separately from the monthly software license subscription cost. Zoho Sign credits are valid until used and don’t have expiration dates.

All subscribers of Zoho Sign paid plans—whether Standard, Professional, Enterprise, API, or Custom—must purchase credits to take certain actions. Credits can only be purchased in increments of 500 at $0.10 per credit (i.e., $50 for 500 credits). In addition, Enterprise plan users receive a certain number of free credits every month, based on the amount the organization is paying for licenses.

Zoho explains that charging for credits is necessary for two reasons. First, the Zoho Sign software relies on many different third-party integrations, which cost money for Zoho to use. Charging for credits is a way to pass this cost on to the user. Second, using credits helps Zoho guard against the abuse of features and services that can be used at a high volume.

The number of credits that users spend depends on the actions that they take. For example:

- Sending a single document for signing via SMS costs 0.5 credits, i.e., $0.05.

- Sending a single document in a bulk send action costs five credits, i.e,. $0.50.

- Timestamping a single document costs two credits, i.e., $0.20.


#### Services needed to implement or manage the platform

As with other enterprise applications, Zoho Sign customers should consider the cost of technical support when evaluating the software’s pricing. Users of Zoho Sign’s Free plan have access to standard email support. Subscribers to paid plans, however, can get priority technical support via email, web, and phone. This support is included with the software’s license fee, so users don’t have to pay extra.

Beyond official support from Zoho, users can also get help from the Zoho Sign community forums and knowledge base.

#### Internal resources needed for ongoing platform management

Because Zoho Sign is a cloud-based service, users likely won’t incur many costs related to managing the platform. All responsibility for the software’s maintenance (including uptime and resolving technical problems) belongs to Zoho, not the customer.

However, organizations that are heavy users of Zoho Sign may still wish to designate a platform administrator to oversee the software. This person would be responsible for setting up and managing Zoho Sign. The role’s responsibilities could include tasks like configuring user accounts and permissions and ensuring the software is being used properly. While this is likely not a full-time job, the costs of this labor should, nevertheless, be factored into the final decision.

Can I use Zoho Sign for free?

Yes, Zoho Sign offers a Free plan, which is best suited for solopreneurs and small businesses. It allows a single user to send up to five documents per month and provides access to basic features such as notifications, mobile and desktop access, integrations with other Zoho apps, and email support.

Does Zoho Sign offer any discounts?

Yes, Zoho Sign offers discounts to certain types of businesses. According to the Zoho Sign pricing page, volume discounts are available. In addition, Zoho says that nonprofit organizations can receive discounted prices and priority technical support.

Does Zoho Sign have nonprofit pricing?

Yes. According to the Zoho Sign pricing page, nonprofit organizations can receive discounted prices and priority technical support.The website does not offer specifics, so interested nonprofits will need to speak with the Zoho sales team for more details.

Is there an alternative to Zoho Sign?

Yes, there are multiple alternatives to Zoho Sign in the e-signature and document management space. Options such as [DocuSign](https://www.capterra.com/p/121506/Docusign/), [DigiSigner](https://www.capterra.com/p/151821/DigiSigner/), and [Xodo Sign](https://www.capterra.com/p/160894/eversign/) have all received high ratings from verified Capterra reviewers, including in the value-for-money category. Other alternatives include [M-Files Ment](https://www.capterra.com/p/122215/M-Files/) and [DocuWare](https://www.capterra.com/p/220466/DocuWare/), although these tools are general-purpose document management tools rather than specialized for e-signatures.

#### Methodology

1. Review excerpts are passages extracted from longer reviews written by verified reviewers. We obtain these excerpts by applying an algorithm that considers factors including, but not limited to, length, topic coverage, and thematic relevance. Excerpts are evaluated for positive or negative sentiment and receive a sentiment score. Excerpts represent user opinion and do not represent the views of, nor constitute, an endorsement by Capterra or its affiliates. Excerpts are not edited for clarity or grammar.

2. Our research team identified these features from vendor websites (as of March 14, 2025) based on their analysis of what users find valuable or expect from digital signature software. This list is not exhaustive. For additional features, refer to the vendor’s website.

web.markdown:
# Zoho Sign for Enterprises

Paperless signing made easy for enterprise businesses

Leverage digital signatures and workflow automation to streamline your operations with the highest level of data security.

[Get started](https://www.zoho.com/sign/signup.html?src=zohosign-for-enterprise) [Contact Sales](https://www.zoho.com/sign/get-quote.html?src=zohosign-for-enterprise)

- ![Gartner](https://www.zohowebstatic.com/sites/zweb/images/otherbrandlogos/gartner.svg)

4.2/5

- ![G2](https://www.zohowebstatic.com/sites/zweb/images/otherbrandlogos/g2.svg)

4.3/5

- ![Appstore](https://www.zohowebstatic.com/sites/zweb/images/otherbrandlogos/appstore.svg)

4.8/5

- ![Google Play](https://www.zohowebstatic.com/sites/zweb/images/otherbrandlogos/google-play.svg)

4.7/5


Zoho Sign offers numerous business benefits, including:

- ##### 80% faster document turnaround time


Get documents signed in minutes or days, not weeks or months.

- ##### $30 saved per envelope


Cut the overall average costs spent on printing, scanning, and mailing documents.

- ##### 50+ hours saved on average per employee


Adopt digital signatures to save time and boost productivity. Automate repeated tasks.


## A secure digital signature application for enterprise businesses

Zoho Sign is a complete digital signature solution for growing businesses around the world. With Zoho Sign, you can sign, send, and manage your business agreements from anywhere.

![secure digital signature](https://www.zohowebstatic.com/sites/zweb/images/sign/secure-digital-signature.png)

- #### Smart single sign-on (SSO)


With SSO authentication, users can access Zoho Sign through their existing corporate identity stores and collaboration suites like Microsoft Active Directory, Google Workspace, and Microsoft 365.

- #### Import documents


Enterprises can quickly [import confidential documents](https://www.zoho.com/sign/features-and-benefits/import-from-content-cloud.html) from their favorite content collaboration apps like Zoho WorkDrive, Dropbox, Box, OneDrive, and Google Drive.

- #### Secure document sharing


Administrators can enable role-based document sharing for users across the organization. Users can easily access and track documents that have been shared with them.

- #### Legal validity


Documents signed using Zoho Sign are [legally valid](https://www.zoho.com/sign/legal-guide.html) and meet the standards of various e-sign laws across the world, such as the ESIGN Act in the US, PIPEDA in Canada, eIDAS in Europe, the ITA-2000 in India, and the ETA in Australia.

- #### Automatic cloud backup


We also provide an option to [automatically back up all signed documents](https://www.zoho.com/sign/features-and-benefits/cloud-content-backup.html) and completion certificates to the cloud storage provider of your choice. Right now, Zoho supports the following cloud storage providers: Zoho WorkDrive, Dropbox, Box, Google Drive, and OneDrive.

- #### Multiple portals


We enable our users to have multiple portals with the same email address in their Zoho Sign accounts for easy management and access control. This way, users have control over their confidential documents.


## Robust security

We ensure that your confidential business documents are secure. We are equipped to handle modern cyberattacks at all levels in our network, system, and processes to ensure that user data is always secure.

![robust security](https://www.zohowebstatic.com/sites/zweb/images/sign/robust-security.png)

- #### Top-tier encryption standards


Zoho Sign is based on public key infrastructure (PKI) to provide the highest level of security for your documents. All your data is protected using AES-256 encryption while at rest and SSL/TLS encryption while in transit.

- #### Multi-factor authentication


Multi-factor authentication via [Zoho OneAuth](https://www.zoho.com/accounts/oneauth.html) serves as an additional layer of security for user accounts. This method requires users to authenticate at two levels to access the application on the web, browser extension, and mobile app.

- #### Regional data centers


Zoho has its own dedicated data centers in secure locations. Access to the data centers is restricted using biometric two-factor authentication.


![Patient centric](https://www.zohowebstatic.com/sites/zweb/images/otherbrandlogos/caresight.png)

### "Zoho Sign has allowed me as a leader to be more efficient when onboarding new employees, contractors, and partners."

**Kenny Schiff**

Founder and Managing Partner, CareSight LLC

## Affordable enterprise-grade features

Signing documents with pen and paper is time-consuming and inefficient. Zoho Sign's Enterprise edition is loaded with features that give signers a seamless digital signature experience.

- ![Collect signatures from hundreds of recipients](https://www.zohowebstatic.com/sites/zweb/images/sign/sign-documents-one-go.png)


#### Collect signatures from hundreds of recipients


The [bulk send](https://www.zoho.com/sign/features-and-benefits/bulk-send.html) feature allows you to send documents to multiple recipients with a single click. All you need to do is upload the CSV file containing all recipient details.

- ![Verify your signer identity](https://www.zohowebstatic.com/sites/zweb/images/sign/verify-identity-government-ids.png)


#### Verify your signer identity with government issued IDs


Ensure the intended individual is actually the one signing the document by [authenticating your signers](https://www.zoho.com/sign/features-and-benefits/signer-authentication.html) with one of the supported eID methods in Zoho Sign.

- ![qualified electronic signatures](https://www.zohowebstatic.com/sites/zweb/images/sign/qualified-electronic-signatures.png)





#### Highest compliance with qualified electronic signatures (QES)



End users can sign document through [eIDAS-compliant qualified signatures](https://www.zoho.com/sign/qualified-electronic-signature-qes.html) without the need to register for a qualified certificate themselves.

- #### Integration with popular apps






  - Zoho CRM
  - HubSpot
  - Vtiger CRM
  - Pipedrive

##### CRM tools

With the Zoho Sign extension, salespeople can streamline recurring sales operations and automate workflows directly from their CRM app.

  - Zoho Books
  - Zoho Inventory
  - Zoho Invoice
  - Zoho Checkout

##### Finance apps

Users can digitally sign invoices, estimates, and credit notes before sending them to customers with the Zoho Sign integration for Zoho Finance apps.

- ![Collect payments and signatures](https://www.zohowebstatic.com/sites/zweb/images/sign/collect-payments-signatures.png)


#### Collect payments and signatures


Say goodbye to manual billing. Get your business documents [signed digitally and collect payments](https://www.zoho.com/sign/sign-and-collect-payments-with-zoho-sign-and-zoho-checkout.html) from your customers at the same time.

- ![Self-service document signing process](https://www.zohowebstatic.com/sites/zweb/images/sign/self-service-signing-process.png)


#### Self-service document signing process


With the [SignForms](https://www.zoho.com/sign/features-and-benefits/signforms.html) feature, create a self-service digital signature process through a secure URL without any coding, and embed the URL anywhere to collect signatures.


## Choose the plan that suits your business

### Professional

$16$16/user/month billed annually

[Get Started](https://www.zoho.com/sign/signup.html?src=zohosign-for-enterprise)

##### Everything in Standard +

- Unlimited documentsExcludes documents sent using bulk send, SignForms, API, high volume automations, custom functions, and advanced integrations across both Zoho and 3rd-party apps.
- _New_ Recipient vernacular configurationPresently supports 18 languages - Arabic, Chinese (Simplified), Czech, Danish, Dutch, English, French, German, Hebrew, Hungarian, Italian, Japanese, Polish, Portuguese, Russian, Slovakian, Spanish, and Swedish.
- Attachments by signers
- Unlimited templates
- Template sharing

- Rebranding, email templates and signature disclosure customization

Sending documents for signature using high volume automations and custom functions incur additional cost. Each such document signature request consumes 5 Zoho Sign credits, an add-on requiring purchase in denominations of 500 at $0 _.10_$0.1 per credit.

- Automation credits\*Monthly credits awarded on a complimentary basis to send documents for signature via high volume automations and custom functions across Zoho apps and integrations. Each such document signature request consumes an automation credit. Upon exhaustion, subsequent usage will incur additional cost. Each subsequent document signature request will consume 5 Zoho Sign credits, an add-on requiring purchase in denominations of 500 at $0 _.10_$0.1 per credit.

web.markdown:
# Zoho Sign Credits - Add-on **Available only in the paid plans** Zoho Sign allows users to sign or send unlimited documents for e-signature through its user interface functions in the Professional and Enterprise plans across both the web and mobile apps. This has helped transform tens of thousands of businesses around the globe in going paperless and digitizing their documents. However, some features/integrations of the app incur additional cost when used regardless of a users' subscription plan. This additional cost is due to: - the feature/integration making use of a third-party service provider - to meet the licensing costs and service charges levied by the provider for their services; - the feature/integration has automation functions that can be triggered in high volumes and must be subjected to fair use - to limit excessive usage and prevent abuse of the feature against our generous licensing model. This additional cost is charged to users in the form of add-ons which need to be purchased and utilised separately.  Therefore, in Zoho Sign, this is done by offering users the option to purchase Zoho Sign credits, our common add-on, which can be bought and consumed when making use of the described features. Zoho Sign credits can be purchased alongside a Zoho Sign paid subscription, or when renewing a subscription, or separately at any time as per users' convenience. The described features will then consume these credits at varying levels proportionate to the cost they incur or determined by Zoho Sign. These features that require Zoho Sign credits and their respective rate of consumption are listed in a [chart](https://www.zoho.com/sign/zoho-sign-credits.html#consumption-chart) below. **What are Zoho Sign credits and what are they used for?** Zoho Sign credits are a prepaid add-on that require additional purchase on top of the subscription cost of the Zoho Sign general licensing plans. Zoho Sign credits are consumed when users in an organization use features/integrations that incur additional cost. **How much does a Zoho Sign credit cost and how many can I buy?** Zoho Sign credits cost 10 cents or 0.1 USD (you may refer to our [pricing page](https://www.zoho.com/sign/pricing.html) for pricing in regional currencies). They can be purchased in denominations of 500 (e.g. 500 credits for $50 and so on). **Where can I purchase and monitor the usage of Zoho Sign credits?** You can purchase Zoho Sign credits and monitor your organization's usage of them from _Settings > Admin > Subscription details_ in the Zoho Sign web app. **Do I have to purchase Zoho Sign credits for all automation features and integrations?** Not really, it depends on your Zoho Sign subscription. Users in the Enterprise plan get monthly [complimentary Zoho Sign credits](https://www.zoho.com/sign/complimentary-credits.html) based on the number of licenses they have purchased. When using high volume automations, custom functions, and advanced integrations within Zoho, these complimentary credits are first consumed until they are exhausted. They will only require the purchase of Zoho Sign credits after the exhaustion of complimentary credits. However, these complimentary credits are slightly different from Zoho Sign credits and are only used for automation functions, that too within Zoho. Features that use third-party service providers and automations will always require Zoho Sign credits to work. Users in the Standard and Professional plans must still purchase Zoho Sign credits to execute all high volume automations and advanced integrations. **Who will be eligible to purchase and use Zoho Sign credits?** All organizations subscribed to a paid Zoho Sign license—Standard, Professional, Enterprise, API, or a Zoho bundle that includes Zoho Sign—Zoho One, Zoho PeoplePlus, Zoho Contracts, will be able to purchase and use Zoho Sign credits for the features and integrations supported in their plan or bundle. **Will I be notified when I am running low on Zoho Sign credits?** Yes, you can set reminders that notify you when the Zoho Sign credits left in your account reach a low and a critical threshold. You will be able to define these thresholds based on usage in your organization's [_Subscription details_](https://sign.zoho.com/zs#/admin/license) page. **How long are the Zoho Sign credits I purchase valid for?** The Zoho Sign credits you purchase are valid until they are consumed and do not have an expiry date. ## **Credits Consumption Chart** | **Automation/integration action** | **Zoho Sign credits consumed** | | --- | --- | | Sending documents for signatures via SMS | 0.5 credit per SMS | | Sending authentication code or One-time Password (OTP) via SMS | 0.5 credit per SMS | | Sending documents for signatures using bulk send | 5 credits per recipient | | Receiving submissions on SignForms | 5 credits per response | | Sending documents for signatures using REST APIs and SDKs | 5 credits per document signature request | | Sending documents for signatures using integration automations and custom functions from other Zoho apps/services | 5 credits per document signature request | | Sending documents for signatures using integration automations from third-party apps or services | 5 credits per document signature request | | Associating user with third-party provider - eMudhra eSign | 100 credits per user per year | | Signing documents via third-party provider - Aadhaar eSign | 2 credits per signing activity | | Associating user with QES provider - Uanataca | 200 credits per user per year | | Signing documents via QES provider - Uanataca | 3 credits per signing activity | | Signing documents via AES provider - IgniSign | 4 credits per signing activity | | Associating user with QES provider - InfoCert | 500 credits per user  for 3 years | | Signing documents via QES provider - InfoCert | 3 credits per signing activity | | Signing documents using QES via EU eID - Evrotrust/sign-me (D-Trust)/A-Trust/certME | 18 credits per signing activity | | Signing documents using QES via EU eID - Denmark MitID/Swedish BankID | 18 credits per signing activity | | Signing documents using QES via EU eID - Smart ID/National ID/Mobile ID/eParaksts eID & mobile/CertEurope USB (InfoCert)/certSIGN USB/Trans Sped/Czech ID Card/Croatian ID Card | 4 credits per signing activity | | Signing documents using QES via EU eID - Belgian ID card | 8 credits per signing activity | | Signing documents using QES via EU eID - InfoCert (Austria) | 500 credits for registration

We take your privacy seriously and use our own tools hosted in our data centres. Your data is yours, and we never monetize it for advertisement purposes. You can manage your preference anytime by visiting our [cookie\\ policy](https://www.zoho.com/privacy/cookie-policy.html?src=cp-popup) or by clicking on the cookie icon at the bottom-left corner of the webpage. ##### Strictly Necessary They are necessary for our website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are essential in order to enable you to navigate around the website and use its features. If you remove or disable these cookies from your browser, we cannot guarantee that you will be able to use our websites. [Learn\\ More](https://www.zoho.com/privacy/cookie-policy.html?src_tab=sn) ##### Functional/Preference They allow us to remember the choices made by you (such as your user name, language or region) as well as other functionalities (such as controlling the cookie banner, redirection to a new page) in order to provide a more personalised online experience. These preferences are remembered (through the use of persistent cookies) so that you need not set them again the next time you visit the page. Note : The **live chat** widget will **not** work if the functionality/preference setting is disabled. [Learn\\ More](https://www.zoho.com/privacy/cookie-policy.html?src_tab=fp) ##### Analytics They help us improve the way our websites work (e.g. by ensuring that users are finding what they are looking for easily). These collect aggregate information about visits, navigations in the websites so that we can make improvement and report our performance. Analytics cookies may also be used to test new pages or features to understand how users adopt them. [Learn\\ More](https://www.zoho.com/privacy/cookie-policy.html?src_tab=an) ##### Third-party company cookies When you visit some of our webpages that contain embedded content hosted on a third party platform such as YouTube videos, these third parties set cookies on your browser. We always opt for the most privacy friendly options provided by these parties, however these are not controlled by us. If you do not want these parties to set cookies on your browser on visiting our site, you should avoid visiting webpages that contain such embeds.
